content: Diagnosis requires imaging and tissue confirmation. Screening mammography detects non-palpable cancers; diagnostic mammography + ultrasound for symptomatic patients. MRI for high-risk screening, staging dense breasts, evaluating implant integrity, assessing response to neoadjuvant therapy. Biopsy methods: core needle biopsy preferred (preserves architecture, allows biomarker testing); FNA for lymph nodes; surgical excision if percutaneous not feasible. Pathology: histologic type (ductal, lobular, others); grade (Nottingham score - tubule formation, nuclear pleomorphism, mitotic rate); biomarkers by IHC - ER, PR (positive if ≥1% cells staining), HER2 (IHC 3+ or FISH amplified); Ki-67 proliferation index. Staging: TNM 8th edition incorporates biomarkers into prognostic stage groups. Genomic assays: Oncotype DX (21-gene recurrence score) predicts chemotherapy benefit in HR+ node-negative and some node-positive disease; MammaPrint (70-gene signature) classifies low vs high risk. Germline testing recommended for TNBC <60 years, metastatic disease, strong family history, male breast cancer.
